memoryArtifacts.headline: Bohun Coat of Arms
memoryArtifacts.description:
The surname of the De bohun family begins after the Norman Conquest of 1066. They lived in Sussex. Their name, however, does not refer to the area, but to their former place of residence, the town of Bohun, in the French maritime department of La Manche, in western Normandy.

The surname De bohun was first found in Sussex, where they had been granted lands by King William after the Norman Conquest in 1066.

Known variations of the De bohun family name include Bohon, Bohun, Bone, Boon, Boone, Bohan, Bound and many more.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Earl Humphrey IV de Bohun於1208年出生在英國維爾爾格福德時，他的父親Henry de Bohun 1st Earl of Hereford33歲，母親Maud FitzGeoffrey Countess of Hereford and Essex18歲。他於大約1222年與Maud de Lusignan在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他於1239年被列為sheriff of kent。他去世於1275年9月24日在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾沃里克沃里克 卡素享年67歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾普斯泰德。

nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bohun
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Moon
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Moyon in La Manche. Compare   Mounce  .
Americanized form of German   Mohn   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

nameMeanings.name: Humphrey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Norman name, Hunfrid, of Germanic origin, derived from hun ‘bear-cub, warrior’ + fred, frid ‘peace’. The Norman form absorbed the native Old English form, Hunfrith, which existed in England before the Conquest. The spelling with -ph- reflects classicizing influence. Since the 16th century it has enjoyed a modest popularity in England, having been borne most notably by the youngest son of King Henry IV , the Duke of Gloucester ( 1391–1447 ), known as ‘Duke Humphrey’. He was noted as a patron of literature, and founded what became the Bodleian Library at Oxford. In modern times, probably the most famous bearer has been the film star Humphrey Bogart ( 1899–1957 ).

variants.name: Humphry
meanings: From a Norman name, Hunfrid, of Germanic origin, derived from hun ‘bear-cub, warrior’ + fred, frid ‘peace’. The Norman form absorbed the native Old English form, Hunfrith, which existed in England before the Conquest. The spelling with -ph- reflects classicizing influence. Since the 16th century it has enjoyed a modest popularity in England, having been borne most notably by the youngest son of King Henry IV , the Duke of Gloucester ( 1391–1447 ), known as ‘Duke Humphrey’. He was noted as a patron of literature, and founded what became the Bodleian Library at Oxford. In modern times, probably the most famous bearer has been the film star Humphrey Bogart ( 1899–1957 ).

meanings: From a Norman name, Hunfrid, of Germanic origin, derived from hun ‘bear-cub, warrior’ + fred, frid ‘peace’. The Norman form absorbed the native Old English form, Hunfrith, which existed in England before the Conquest. The spelling with -ph- reflects classicizing influence. Since the 16th century it has enjoyed a modest popularity in England, having been borne most notably by the youngest son of King Henry IV , the Duke of Gloucester ( 1391–1447 ), known as ‘Duke Humphrey’. variants.name: Humfry
meanings: From a Norman name, Hunfrid, of Germanic origin, derived from hun ‘bear-cub, warrior’ + fred, frid ‘peace’. The Norman form absorbed the native Old English form, Hunfrith, which existed in England before the Conquest. The spelling with -ph- reflects classicizing influence. Since the 16th century it has enjoyed a modest popularity in England, having been borne most notably by the youngest son of King Henry IV , the Duke of Gloucester ( 1391–1447 ), known as ‘Duke Humphrey’. He was noted as a patron of literature, and founded what became the Bodleian Library at Oxford. In modern times, probably the most famous bearer has been the film star Humphrey Bogart ( 1899–1957 ).

nameMeanings.name: IV
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Earl Humphrey IV de Bohun was born in 1208, in Hungerford, Wiltshire, England, his father, Henry de Bohun 1st Earl of Hereford, was 33 and his mother, Maud FitzGeoffrey Countess of Hereford and Essex, was 18. He married Maud de Lusignan about 1222, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 4 daughters. In 1239, his occupation is listed as sheriff of kent. He died on 24 September 1275, in Warwick Castle, Warwick, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 67, and was buried in Hempsted, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Earl Humphrey IV de Bohun est né en 1208, à Hungerford, Wiltshire, Angleterre, son père, Henry de Bohun 1st Earl of Hereford, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Maud FitzGeoffrey Countess of Hereford and Essex, avait 18 ans. Il a épousé Maud de Lusignan environ 1222, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 4 filles. En 1239, sa profession de son indiquée est sheriff of kent. Il est décédé le 24 septembre 1275, à Warwick Castle, Warwick, Warwickshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 67 ans, et a été enterré à Hempsted, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Er hat um 1222 in England, United Kingdom Maud de Lusignan geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 4 Töchter. 1239 ist sein Beruf als sheriff of kent aufgeführt. Er starb am 24. September 1275 im Alter von 67 Jahren in Warwick Castle, Warwick, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Hempsted, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Earl Humphrey IV de Bohun è nato il nel 1208 nel Hungerford, Wiltshire, England, il suo padre, Henry de Bohun 1st Earl of Hereford, aveva 33 anni e il suo madre, Maud FitzGeoffrey Countess of Hereford and Essex, aveva 18 anni. Lui sposato Maud de Lusignan circa 1222, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 4 figlie. Nel 1239, la sua occupazione è indicata come sheriff of kent. Morì il 24 settembre 1275, nel Warwick Castle, Warwick, Warwickshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 67 anni, e fu sepolto a Hempsted, Gloucestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Earl Humphrey IV de Bohunが1208年にイギリスウィルトシュアイアハンジャーフォードで生まれた時，父Henry de Bohun 1st Earl of Herefordは33歳，母Maud FitzGeoffrey Countess of Hereford and Essexは18歳であった。約1222年にイギリスイギリスでMaud de Lusignanと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。1239年，職業はsheriff of kentと記録されている。彼は1275年9月24日にイギリスイギリスワーウィクシュアイアワーウィックワーウィック カストルで67歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアヘンプステドにて埋葬された。

ko.Warwick Castle-Warwick, Warwickshire, ,: 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 워윅 워윅 카스틀
ko.lifeSummary: Earl Humphrey IV de Bohun(이)가 1208년 영국 윌츠히르 헌그르퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Henry de Bohun 1st Earl of Hereford(은)는 33세였고, 그의 어머니 Maud FitzGeoffrey Countess of Hereford and Essex(은)는 18세였다. 그는 대략 1222년 영국 잉글랜드에서 Maud de Lusignan(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 1239년, 그의 직업은 sheriff of kent이었다. 그는 1275년 9월 24일 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 워윅 워윅 카스틀에서 67세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 헤프스테드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Earl Humphrey IV de Bohun nasceu em 1208, em Hungerford, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Henry de Bohun 1st Earl of Hereford, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Maud FitzGeoffrey Countess of Hereford and Essex, tinha 18 anos. Ele casou-se com Maud de Lusignan aproximadamente 1222, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 4 filhas. Em 1239, sua profissão aparece como sheriff of kent. Ele faleceu em 24 de setembro de 1275, em Warwick Castle, Warwick, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 67 anos, e foi sepultado em Hempsted, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.Warwick Castle-Warwick, Warwickshire, ,: варвикк кастл, варвикк, варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Earl Humphrey IV de Bohun родился в 1208 г. в местности хунгирфорд, уилцхир, Англия, его отцу по имени Henry de Bohun 1st Earl of Hereford было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Maud FitzGeoffrey Countess of Hereford and Essex, 18. Он женился на женщине по имени Maud de Lusignan около 1222 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 4 дочери. В 1239 году он работал по профессии sheriff of kent. Он умер 24 сентября 1275 г. в местности варвикк кастл, варвикк, варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 67 лет и был похоронен в местности хемпстед, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Earl Humphrey IV de Bohun nació en 1208, en Hungerford, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Henry de Bohun 1st Earl of Hereford, tenía 33 años y su mamá, Maud FitzGeoffrey Countess of Hereford and Essex, tenía 18 años. Se casó con Maud de Lusignan aproximadamente 1222, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 4 hijas. En 1239, su ocupación registrada era sheriff of kent. Murió el 24 de septiembre de 1275, en Warwick Castle, Warwick, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 67 años, y fue sepultado en Hempsted, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
